body.title: Research: Google AppScript as GoogleSecretary substrate
body.result: ADOPT AppScript Web App for write ops; decision doc at D-google-appscript-2026-06-04.md
body.description:
Leah built an app using Google AppScript that reads Sheets and sends email server-side. AppScript runs inside Google's infrastructure, has native access to all Workspace APIs (Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Drive), and doesn't require OAuth dance for each operation — the script runs as the authenticated user. This may be a more durable substrate for GoogleSecretary than browser automation via SWADL, which is fragile to UI changes. Research ticket: evaluate AppScript as the execution layer for GoogleSecretary operations and produce a decision doc.

**Affected files:** lab/design_docs/decisions/D-google-appscript-YYYY-MM-DD.md (output artifact)
**Design rules:** none apply — research/decision doc only
**Scope boundary:** In scope: what AppScript can do (APIs, triggers, quotas), how to call it from a Python rack device (Apps Script API via service account or webhook), comparison with SWADL approach, decision recommendation. Out of scope: implementation of AppScript functions, wiring into GoogleSecretary device.
**Completion criteria:** Decision doc written at lab/design_docs/decisions/ covering: AppScript capability surface, invocation path from Python, quota limits, verdict (adopt/defer/reject) with rationale
